meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solve Incomplete Apps
content.seo_description: Stop leaving projects unfinished. Master project management with this advanced guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship real-world apps.
content.answer_block: To stop leaving projects unfinished, you must decompose scope into atomic, high-impact features. Use Antigravity to architect your MVP and Claude Code to automate implementation. By treating your codebase as a series of shippable, 4-hour sprints, you maintain momentum, hit learning milestones, and build a portfolio that actually gets you hired.
content.intro:
You sit before a blank IDE, the weight of a 'perfect' app idea pressing down on you. You write a few lines, get lost in the weeds of a framework, and suddenly, the project is just another ghost in your GitHub repository. The graveyard of unfinished side projects isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of structural discipline.

Today, we pivot from hobbyist tinkering to professional-grade engineering. By marrying the rapid prototyping of Antigravity with the autonomous execution of Claude Code, you will transform vague ambitions into production-ready software. This is your blueprint for moving from 'student' to 'ship-ready engineer.'

steps.title: Define the Atomic MVP
steps.description: Narrow your scope to the single core feature that provides value. If it isn't essential to the user's primary goal, delete it from the roadmap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and decompose it into a 3-day MVP roadmap. Focus on the core user flow and provide a schema for the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP scope defined by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Layer
steps.description: Solidify your database schema before writing a single line of business logic. A weak foundation leads to refactoring hell.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema based on my MVP features. Ensure relationships are normalized and support the core user requirements.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the prisma schema file based on the provided SQL architecture.'

steps.title: Automate Feature Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate the boilerplate code for your API routes and controllers to maintain consistent patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a directory structure and file templates for my backend API services, adhering to clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the API route structure for the user authentication and core data service modules.'

steps.title: Implement Iterative Logic
steps.description: Build features sequentially. Use Claude Code to write tests before implementing the logic to ensure high code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a TDD (Test-Driven Development) plan for the primary feature set. Provide the unit tests for the first module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Write tests for the current service module and implement the code to pass them.'

steps.title: Refactor for Production
steps.description: Before deploying, use your tools to identify technical debt and optimize for performance and security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on the codebase. Identify security vulnerabilities and suggest optimizations for production readiness.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the codebase based on the security and performance audit provided.'

pro_tips: Never build without a 'Stop-Loss' date; if a feature isn't working by the deadline, cut it or simplify it.
Use Claude Code to write your documentation as you code, not after; it keeps the context fresh.
Treat your GitHub commit history as a professional narrative; one feature, one clean pull request.
If you feel stuck, use Antigravity to explain the code back to you in plain English to identify mental gaps.
Prioritize deploying a 'Hello World' to production on day one to solve deployment hurdles early.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is deployed, document your learning process in a blog post and share it on LinkedIn to build your professional brand.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas rot in a folder. Master the workflow that turns students into ship-ready developers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
